content:
# Final Review Planner Positions
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Evaluated three amendments to the success reconciliation feature. Amendment fr-17-2f3210bd (persist run_id on DaemonTask) identifies a real correctness bug where concurrent project activity can cause reconciliation against the wrong run — accepted. Amendment fr-17-a299d694 (bypass worktree for reconciliation retries) identifies a real robustness issue where reconciliation-only retries needlessly go through worktree creation and remote resume, risking unrelated failures — accepted. Amendment fr-17-dde66e40 (RunResumed fallback) raises a valid but low-severity concern; since journals are append-only and RunStarted always precedes RunResumed, the existing code is correct, but a clarifying comment is warranted — accepted as a minor hardening measure.

## Votes

- `fr-17-dde66e40`: Accept
  The amendment correctly observes that try_reconcile_success only searches for RunStarted events when extracting run_id. Since journals are append-only and RunStarted always precedes any RunResumed for the same run, the current code works correctly — the original RunStarted event is always present. However, the amendment's suggestion to add a comment documenting this invariant (option a) is good practice. The RunResumed event also contains the run_id in its details JSON, so adding it as a fallback (option b) would be cheap defensive coding against hypothetical journal truncation. Either option improves maintainability at minimal cost and risk. Accept as a minor hardening measure.
- `fr-17-a299d694`: Accept
  This identifies a real robustness issue. The is_reconciliation_retry guard in the daemon loop (lines 649-696 and 1412-1446) only skips rebase/ensure_project steps, but claim_task has already run LeaseService::acquire which creates a worktree and calls try_resume_from_remote for failed-task retries. For reconciliation-specific failure classes (BrCloseFailed, BrSyncFailed, MilestoneUpdateFailed), the only remaining work is br close/sync plus milestone bookkeeping — none of which requires a worktree checkout or remote branch resume. If worktree creation or remote resume fails (disk space, network, auth issues), the reconciliation retry fails before ever reaching try_reconcile_success, even though it doesn't need those resources. The fix should either detect reconciliation failure classes before calling claim_task and use a lightweight claim path, or pass the failure class into claim_task/LeaseService::acquire so they can skip worktree operations for reconciliation-only retries.
- `fr-17-2f3210bd`: Accept
  This is a genuine correctness bug. DaemonTask (model.rs) stores only project_id and requirements_run_id — it has no field for the workflow run_id. try_reconcile_success reconstructs the run_id by finding the most recent RunStarted event in the project journal. If the project is manually rerun or resumed between the original failure and the retry, the journal will contain a newer RunStarted event for a different run. The reconciliation code would then attach the task_id to the wrong run, write incorrect completion timestamps, and perform lineage repair against the wrong workflow execution. This is not a theoretical concern — operator intervention between failure and retry is exactly the scenario reconciliation is designed for. Storing the workflow run_id on DaemonTask when dispatch starts (alongside the existing requirements_run_id pattern) and reading it in try_reconcile_success instead of scanning the journal is the correct fix.
